claim_boundary: Challenge, insufficient-evidence, or escalation outcomes are risk-triage signals; they are not automatic proof that the model identified semantic incorrectness.

cases.challenge_reason: Single passing test does not validate filename trimming logic, edge cases, or regression on existing download functionality.
cases.primary_reason: Only one visible fail-to-pass test passed, no regression tests or broader diagnostics visible, insufficient evidence to confirm patch correctness or absence of regressions.

cases.challenge_reason: Single passing test without regression tests or broader diagnostics does not sufficiently validate the patch's correctness or guard against regressions.
cases.primary_reason: Only one visible fail-to-pass test passed, no regression tests or broader diagnostics visible, insufficient evidence to confirm correctness or absence of regressions.

cases.challenge_reason: Only a single fail-to-pass test passed; no regression tests or broader diagnostics to ensure the patch does not break other functionality.
cases.primary_reason: Only one visible fail-to-pass test passed, no regression tests visible, and no broader diagnostics to confirm correctness or absence of regressions.

cases.challenge_reason: A single passing fail-to-pass test without any regression tests or broader diagnostics is insufficient to validate the patch's correctness and safety.
cases.primary_reason: Only one visible fail-to-pass test passed, with no regression tests or broader diagnostics to confirm the patch does not break other functionality or handle edge cases.

cases.challenge_reason: A single passing fail-to-pass test without any regression tests or broader diagnostics is insufficient to validate the patch, especially given the change affects a core function (get_response) that could impact many behaviors.
cases.primary_reason: Only one visible fail-to-pass test passed, with no regression tests or broader diagnostics to confirm correctness or absence of side effects.

cases.challenge_reason: Single passing test does not sufficiently cover session header update behavior for None values across different scenarios; no regression tests or broader diagnostics confirm patch safety.
cases.primary_reason: Only one visible fail-to-pass test passed, no regression tests or broader diagnostics available, insufficient evidence to confirm patch correctness or absence of regressions.

cases.challenge_reason: A single passing fail-to-pass test without regression tests or broader diagnostics does not sufficiently validate the patch's correctness or guard against regressions.
cases.primary_reason: Only one visible fail-to-pass test passed, with no regression tests or broader diagnostics to confirm correctness or absence of side effects.

cases.challenge_reason: Single passing test does not guarantee the patch handles all cases (e.g., mixed-case headers, multiple Host headers, or other header interactions) and lacks regression coverage.
cases.primary_reason: Only one visible fail-to-pass test passed, with no regression tests or broader diagnostics to confirm the patch does not introduce regressions or handle edge cases.

cases.challenge_reason: Single passing test does not validate filename trimming logic, edge cases, or regression on existing download functionality.
cases.primary_reason: Only one visible fail-to-pass test passed, with no regression tests or broader diagnostics to confirm correctness or absence of side effects.

cases.challenge_reason: The patch introduces complex filename trimming logic but is only validated by a single fail-to-pass test with no regression tests or static analysis to ensure it doesn't break standard download behaviors.
cases.primary_reason: Only one fail-to-pass test is visible and no regression tests or static diagnostics were run, leaving the broader impact of the filename trimming logic unverified.

cases.challenge_reason: A single fail-to-pass test without regression tests or broader diagnostics is insufficient to verify the new OS-dependent filename trimming logic.
cases.primary_reason: Only a single fail-to-pass test is visible with no pass-to-pass regression tests or broader diagnostics to verify the new OS-dependent filename trimming logic.

cases.challenge_reason: The single passing test does not cover cross-platform execution where os.pathconf would raise an unhandled AttributeError on Windows.
cases.primary_reason: Patch uses Unix-only os.pathconf which will raise an unhandled AttributeError on Windows, and lacks pass-to-pass regression tests to verify cross-platform compatibility.

cases.challenge_reason: Validation relies solely on one narrow fail-to-pass test without regression coverage or static checks.
cases.primary_reason: Patch passes the single fail-to-pass test but lacks visible regression tests and broader diagnostics.

cases.challenge_reason: A visible-test-only accept is challenged because the single passing fail-to-pass test does not guarantee the absence of regressions without pass-to-pass tests or static diagnostics.
cases.primary_reason: The patch passes the single fail-to-pass test but lacks pass-to-pass regression tests and broader diagnostics, making the evidence insufficient for a safe merge.

cases.challenge_reason: Only one fail-to-pass test passed with zero regression tests and no static diagnostics, leaving broader session handling unverified.
cases.primary_reason: Patch passes a single fail-to-pass test but lacks pass-to-pass regression tests and broader diagnostics to verify no regressions.

cases.challenge_reason: The patch is validated only by a single fail-to-pass test, lacking pass-to-pass regression tests and broader diagnostics to confirm no unintended side effects.
cases.primary_reason: Visible evidence is limited to a single fail-to-pass test with no regression tests or broader diagnostics to verify the patch's safety.
The patch correctly implements a case-insensitive check for the Host header, directly resolving the duplication issue, and the visible fail-to-pass test confirms the fix.

cases.challenge_reason: A single fail-to-pass test is insufficient to verify the OS-specific pathconf logic and ensure no regressions in download filename handling.
cases.primary_reason: Patch introduces OS-specific os.pathconf without handling AttributeError for non-Unix systems, and lacks visible regression tests.

next_step: Interpret tool-contestation signals separately from strict reject corrections.
